practices.title: Never hardcode secrets in source code
practices.explanation: Hardcoded secrets are committed to version control and exposed in logs. Always read from environment variables or a secrets manager.

standards.title: Credentials and secrets MUST NOT be hardcoded in source files
Credentials MUST be loaded from secure external sources, never hardcoded

practices.title: Use parameterized queries — never string-template SQL

cwe.name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

practices.good: db.query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", listOf(userId))
practices.explanation: String-templated SQL enables injection attacks. Use parameterized queries or an ORM's safe query builder.

standards.title: SQL queries MUST use parameterised statements; string concatenation is forbidden
SQL queries MUST use parameterised statements

Verify that data selection or database queries (e.g. SQL, HQL, ORM, NoSQL) use parameterized queries, ORMs, entity frameworks, or are otherwise protected from database injection attacks. ([C3](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

Verify that where parameterized or safer mechanisms are not present, context-specific output encoding is used to protect against injection attacks, such as the use of SQL escaping to protect against SQL injection. ([C3, C4](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

practices.title: Validate external input before processing
practices.explanation: Unvalidated input leads to injection and data corruption. Validate at API boundaries using require/check.

standards.title: All user input MUST be validated and sanitised before use
All external input MUST be validated against expected format and range

Verify that all input (HTML form fields, REST requests, URL parameters, HTTP headers, cookies, batch files, RSS feeds, etc) is validated using positive validation (allow lists). ([C5](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

Verify that structured data is strongly typed and validated against a defined schema including allowed characters, length and pattern (e.g. credit card numbers, e-mail addresses, telephone numbers, or validating that two related fields are reasonable, such as checking that suburb and zip/postcode match). ([C5](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))
Verify that JSON schema validation is in place and verified before accepting input.
Verify that XSD schema validation takes place to ensure a properly formed XML document, followed by validation of each input field before any processing of that data takes place.

practices.title: Never use Runtime.exec with unsanitized input

cwe.name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

practices.explanation: Constructing shell commands from user input enables command injection. Use ProcessBuilder with argument arrays.

standards.title: Shell commands MUST NOT be constructed from user-controlled data
OS commands MUST NOT be constructed from user-controlled data

Verify that the application protects against OS command injection and that operating system calls use parameterized OS queries or use contextual command line output encoding. ([C4](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))
Verify that untrusted file metadata is not used directly with system API or libraries, to protect against OS command injection.

practices.title: Use data classes for DTOs and value objects
practices.good: data class User(val name: String, val email: String)
practices.explanation: Data classes auto-generate equals, hashCode, toString, and copy. Manual implementations are error-prone and verbose.

standards.title: Wildcard imports (import *) MUST NOT be used
Internal implementation details MUST be hidden behind public interfaces

practices.title: Use sealed classes for restricted type hierarchies
practices.explanation: Sealed classes ensure exhaustive when-expressions and prevent unexpected subtypes from being added outside the file.

standards.title: Wildcard imports (import *) MUST NOT be used
Internal implementation details MUST be hidden behind public interfaces

practices.title: Prefer extension functions over utility classes
practices.explanation: Extension functions are more idiomatic in Kotlin and discoverable via IDE autocomplete. Avoid Java-style static utility classes.

standards.title: Wildcard imports (import *) MUST NOT be used
Internal implementation details MUST be hidden behind public interfaces

practices.title: Keep files under 300 lines

cwe.name: Source Code File with Excessive Number of Lines of Code

practices.bad: // 500-line file mixing data classes, business logic, and API calls
practices.explanation: Large files violate Single Responsibility Principle and are hard to navigate and test.

standards.title: Source files MUST NOT exceed 300 lines
Functions MUST NOT exceed 50 lines
Source files MUST NOT exceed language-appropriate line limits

practices.title: Use runCatching for recoverable operations
practices.explanation: runCatching provides a functional error-handling pattern with map/recover chains. Avoids broad catch blocks.

standards.title: Async operations MUST handle rejection/error paths explicitly
Exceptional conditions MUST be caught and handled appropriately

practices.title: Never swallow exceptions with empty catch blocks

cwe.name: Detection of Error Condition Without Action

practices.good: try { riskyOp() } catch (e: Exception) { logger.error("Failed", e) }
practices.explanation: Empty catch blocks hide errors and make debugging impossible. At minimum log the exception.

standards.title: Exceptions MUST be caught and handled; empty catch blocks are forbidden
All detected error conditions MUST trigger handling logic

practices.title: Handle coroutine exceptions with CoroutineExceptionHandler
practices.explanation: Uncaught coroutine exceptions crash the app or are silently lost. Use structured concurrency with exception handlers.

standards.title: Async operations MUST handle rejection/error paths explicitly
Exceptional conditions MUST be caught and handled appropriately

practices.title: Use safe calls and Elvis operator for nullable handling
practices.explanation: Force unwrap (!!) crashes at runtime on null. Use safe calls (?.) and Elvis (?:) for null-safe access.

standards.title: Null/undefined dereferences MUST be guarded before use
Nullable references MUST be checked before dereferencing

practices.title: Use sequences for large collection pipelines
practices.explanation: Collection operations create intermediate lists at each step. Sequences evaluate lazily, processing one element at a time.

standards.title: Unbounded result sets MUST be paginated or limited
Database connections MUST use connection pooling
Large collections MUST use lazy iteration (generators, streams) instead of full materialization
Caches MUST have eviction policies; unbounded caches are forbidden
Resource consumption MUST be bounded with limits and timeouts

practices.title: Use appropriate coroutine dispatchers

cwe.name: Synchronous Access of Remote Resource without Timeout

practices.explanation: IO-bound work on Main dispatcher blocks the UI thread. Use Dispatchers.IO for file/network operations and Default for CPU-bound work.

standards.title: Sequential awaits on independent async calls MUST be parallelised
Synchronous blocking I/O MUST NOT be used in async contexts
Remote resource access MUST use timeouts and async patterns where available

practices.title: Avoid unnecessary object allocation in hot paths

cwe.name: Excessive Platform Resource Consumption within a Loop

practices.good:
fun formatItems(items: List<Item>): String =
    items.joinToString(",") { it.name }
practices.explanation: String concatenation in loops creates O(n) intermediate String objects. Use joinToString or StringBuilder.

standards.title: Resource allocation inside loops MUST be minimised; pre-allocate where possible
Resource allocation inside loops MUST be minimised; pre-allocate where possible

practices.title: Avoid N+1 queries — batch database access

cwe.name: Invokable Control Element with Excessive File or Data Access Operations

practices.good:
val ids = users.map { it.id }
val orders = orderRepo.findByUserIds(ids)
practices.explanation: Querying inside a loop creates N+1 round-trips. Batch into a single query with IN clause.

standards.title: Database queries inside loops are forbidden (N+1 problem)
String concatenation in loops MUST use a builder or join pattern
Hot-path code MUST NOT perform redundant computation; cache or memoize results
Data access operations MUST be batched; no queries inside loops
